creator: guru@unixarea.de
text:
The culprit must be this part of the script

...
mkimg -s mbr \
    -b ${BASEBITSDIR}/boot/mbr \
    -p efi:=${espfilename} \
    -p freebsd:-"mkimg -s bsd -b ${BASEBITSDIR}/boot/boot -p freebsd-ufs:=${2}.part" \
    -a 2 \
    -o ${2}

I saw the two processes 'mkimg' running, the inner reading the two files '/tmp/mkimg-oGNnFb' and 'memstick.img.part' (22G) and some other file in /tmp was written to, but not nothing to 'memstick.img'. 

It looks like the inner process reading the two files did not ended on EOF and send bytes of zero or garbage into the pipe to the outer mkimg which made it never ending. One should run it again and watch what will happen. 

I'm off until September 4.
